products.name: DHK4A180
products.descriptor: 180 hp FAA-certified jet-fueled piston aircraft engine

evidence.snippet: (Racine, Wisconsin) The stroke of an official pen and the presentation of appropriate documents marked a milestone years in the making – as DeltaHawk Engines has received FAA Type Certification for its highly innovative, jet-fueled aircraft piston engine. The 180 horsepower DHK4A180 is the first of a family of engines to come from DeltaHawk.

DeltaHawk Engines has added two new higher horsepower engine models to its family of jet-fueled piston engines. Both of the new engines are based upon the architecture of the company’s highly innovative DHK4A180, which was FAA Certified in April 2023 and is now being readied for production ahead of anticipated deliveries in 2025.

products.notes: FAA Type Certified April 2023; production ramp-up underway, first deliveries expected 2026 per latest news. Made in USA badge on site.

products.name: DHK4A200
products.descriptor: 200 hp jet-fueled piston aircraft engine, certification slated 2026

evidence.snippet: Testing is underway at DeltaHawk Engines as two new higher horsepower versions of its FAA Certified DHK4A180 engine are introduced. The 200 horsepower DHK4A200 is slated for certification and delivery in 2026 followed by the 235 horsepower DHK4A235.

products.notes: Reservations open; shares dimensions/weight with DHK4A180; certification and delivery targeted 2026.

products.name: DHK4A235
products.descriptor: 235 hp jet-fueled piston aircraft engine, certification following DHK4A200

evidence.snippet: Testing is underway at DeltaHawk Engines as two new higher horsepower versions of its FAA Certified DHK4A180 engine are introduced. The 200 horsepower DHK4A200 is slated for certification and delivery in 2026 followed by the 235 horsepower DHK4A235.

products.notes: Reservations open; shares dimensions/weight with DHK4A180; certification follows DHK4A200 (post-2026).

products.name: DHK Engine Family (Government/Military Application)

evidence.snippet: [Read the article](https://www.ust-media.com/ust-magazine/UST054/64/) or learn more about the [military applications](https://www.deltahawk.com/military-applications/) of the DeltaHawk engine.
